title: SIEM_THREAT_LAB
subtitle: Enterprise-grade SIEM Deployment & Log Analysis
problem: Traditional security monitoring in lab environments lacked centralized visibility for anomalous event correlation.
action: Deployed and configured a Splunk-based SIEM environment with sysmon integration for real-time Windows security log ingestion.
result: Reduced mean-time-to-detection (MTTD) for simulated brute-force attacks by 45% through custom alert correlation rules.
constraints: Required low-latency log forwarding over high-traffic virtual subnets.
failures: Initial log forwarding configurations caused data overflow; resolved by implementing universal forwarders with local buffering.

title: SENTINEL_IDS
subtitle: Real-time Network Intrusion Detection Engine
problem: Standard perimeter defenses failed to capture lateral movement within the internal lab subnet.
action: Engineered a Scapy-driven IDS with signature-matching for unusual TCP handshake patterns and Nmap scanning signatures.
result: Achieved 92% detection accuracy for common discovery and enumeration techniques.
constraints: High network throughput required efficient zero-copy packet processing.
failures: Primary packet queue encountered buffer overflows; mitigated by implementing multi-threaded deque handling.

title: PHISH_TRIAGE_AUTO
subtitle: Automated Phishing Analysis Pipeline
problem: Manual analysis of suspicious emails was time-consuming and inconsistent during high-intensity SOC simulations.
action: Developed a Python framework to parse .eml files, extract headers, and verify URL reputation via VirusTotal/Urlscan APIs.
result: Automated the initial assessment for 100% of incoming suspicious email samples in the test dataset.
